title_guess: North Wales: Roman Steps, Rhinog, Harlech

pages.text:
Grandma, Grandpa + Aunt Helen came to lunch. In the afternoon we heard the orchestra + Thomas played the solo in Haydn's G Major Concerto 1st Movement. Very exciting. Lovely.

Grandma Brought a birthday cake for John which we had after the concert.

August 26.
4.50. We left S'ham with [?Salrad] [?Bernat], Thomas' French exchange. He + Thomas shared a tent. We heard Peter play his viola in the last quartet, Haydn. Grandma + Grandpa + Helen came too, bringing William to babysit. Spider, the dog, has gouged himself badly jumping over barbed wire.
Sat 27.
Also Duncan offered us a drink, but we were already in bed.
We walked up to Churcham church, past the fields of beans, picking blackberries. Peter played in the orchestra, der Freischutz was his favourite. Thomas was relieved that Peter had not broken his [?shara] torch.
[strikethrough: Friday] Sat Sept 3rd
	We left at 8.30. + breakfasted at the Wolverhampton camping shop where Simon bought stools.
	1.55. We reached [?Limes] + had lunch. The rain started. We sat in the pyramid + read + played draughts. The boys went up the hill, Simon followed

to scare them; but was spotted. They all got wet. It was lovely + cosy in the tent.

Sun Sept 4.
	The weather forecast said clear in the morning: rain in the afternoon, so we decided to walk up the Roman Steps in the morning, + go to Evensong in the evening. The heather is very beautiful at this time of year. We had lunch at the top of the Roman Steps. Rain started as we finished. William, Charles + Eve went back to the van. Simon, Thomas, Peter + John set off for Rhinog. But the rain came sweeping in in gusts. They arrived back only 1½ hours after us, after a shortened trip. We had [?2] bags of wet clothes. Only one change left.
	We went to Harlech. At 5.30. There was a Evensong in Welsh. William had a rebellious tantrums that he wouldn't go to church, and would walk out "at the high point of the Sermon". As nearly all the service was in Welsh, none of us could identify any point in the Sermon, except for "Desmond Tutu", and "preach me another sermon so I can fall asleep". Moreover, the

old people in church invited us into the choir stalls, & an old lady sat next to William & helped him through every page of the dual service books. They were very welcoming.
	Afterwards we had a delicious supper in the restaurant across the road. We had sticky toffee cake with butterscotch sauce.
Sat Mon 5th Sept
	The sky is a mixture of bright blue & dark clouds. The colours of the trees & long grass are very rich. We messed about at the campsite in the morning. We had forgotten the newspaper, so Simon has trouble getting the bar-b-que going. It almost rained, and then, to our amazement, we had hot sunshine from a blue sky in the afternoon. We ambled up the hill behind the campsite & William took photos. The boys roamed & played by the stream in the afternoon.
	This is my favourite campsite.
	Peter tries to make a water mill. When the sun went down at about 7, it became very cold.
	William + Peter keep fighting - Simon took down the big tent.
Tues 6th Sept.
	It showered in the night & early morning. We left at 10.15.
	On the way home we stopped at Machynlleth Centre for Alternative Technology. But the boys started fighting yet again in the carpark. So Simon put away the [?traincoats] & said 'do up yr seatbelts' & we went [home?]
